title: Mighty Mouse
shortOutcome: An evaluation-driven harness for finding faster, more reliable ways to run small local coding models.
ownership: Original evaluation harness, benchmark workflow, protocol experiments, verification gates, and CLI. Model runtimes and foundation models are third-party systems.

evidence.label: Paired validation
evidence.detail: 15 baseline and 15 Lean Protocol runs across the promotion suite.

evidence.label: Verified reliability
evidence.detail: Both cohorts passed every verification gate in the 30-trial validation.
evidence.limitation: This result applies to the recorded benchmark suite, configuration, and environment—not every model or coding task.

evidence.label: Average speedup
evidence.detail: Average latency improvement for Lean versus baseline across the paired validation.

evidence.label: Separate mini-spike
evidence.detail: Average execution-time reduction across three fixed-environment tasks in an earlier, smaller spike.
evidence.limitation: A three-task exploratory result reported separately from the 30-trial promotion validation.

problem.title: Small models can be capable and still fail the workflow.
body: Local coding models are attractive because they are private, inexpensive, and available without a network dependency. The hard part is not getting one impressive answer; it is producing useful changes repeatedly under real file, format, scope, and verification constraints.

Mighty Mouse turns prompt and harness decisions into controlled experiments. The same tasks run through competing configurations, and promotion depends on verified outcomes rather than subjective output quality.

ownership.title: The harness is the product; the models are replaceable inputs.
body: I designed and built the orchestration, task fixtures, response parsing, benchmark runners, telemetry, promotion gates, safety checks, and command-line interface.

The system runs third-party local model runtimes and foundation models. I am not claiming authorship of those models; the work is in making their behavior measurable, configurable, and operationally useful.

systemMap.label: Task fixture
systemMap.detail: A scoped coding problem with expected files and assertions.

systemMap.label: Candidate config
systemMap.detail: Model, protocol, prompt format, limits, and tool policy.

systemMap.label: Isolated workspace
systemMap.detail: Each run receives controlled files and hidden evaluator state.

systemMap.label: Runner + parser
systemMap.detail: Executes the candidate and converts output into a deterministic change.

systemMap.label: Verification gate
systemMap.detail: Tests, schema, scope, and mandatory-format checks decide success.

systemMap.label: Promotion record
systemMap.detail: Latency, tokens, pass rate, and regressions become an auditable decision.

systemMap.label: Abort / quarantine
systemMap.detail: Parser errors, unsafe scope, or failed tests stop promotion.

decisions.title: Verification outranks eloquence
decisions.decision: Score changed files and executable checks instead of grading the model’s explanation.
decisions.consequence: Configurations can be compared on the result that matters: whether the task was completed safely and correctly.

decisions.title: Remove reasoning when evidence permits
decisions.decision: The Lean Protocol skips expensive planning behavior for task classes where verification proves it unnecessary.
decisions.consequence: The promoted configuration improved average latency without reducing the recorded pass rate.

decisions.title: Treat parser failures as system failures
decisions.decision: Output contracts, hidden state, fallbacks, and telemetry became first-class harness concerns.
decisions.consequence: A model response is not called successful unless it can survive the path from text to verified change.

buildRecord.label: 01 / CONTROL
buildRecord.title: Reproducible task suite
buildRecord.detail: Baseline and candidate configurations execute the same versioned tasks in isolated workspaces.

buildRecord.label: 02 / OBSERVE
buildRecord.title: Per-run telemetry
buildRecord.detail: Latency, token use, parser state, changed files, assertions, and failure categories are captured.

buildRecord.label: 03 / DECIDE
buildRecord.title: Promotion protocol
buildRecord.detail: Candidates must retain reliability, pass replay gates, and clear safety checks before becoming defaults.

buildRecord.label: 04 / OPERATE
buildRecord.title: CLI and diagnostics
buildRecord.detail: A compact command surface runs doctor checks, demos, benchmarks, and result inspection.

failures.title: Decomposition produced parser-contract failures
failures.observed: An early decomposition strategy returned output the harness could not apply, producing zero-second parser failures.
failures.response: Standardized stdout, isolated hidden state, added metadata fallbacks, and captured per-pass telemetry before reevaluation.

failures.title: One network task regressed under Lean
failures.observed: The validation watchlist recorded a 37.6% latency regression for task_005 even though the task passed.
failures.response: Kept the result visible as a monitoring item rather than averaging it away.

failures.title: Broader local-model claims remain unproven
failures.observed: Current evidence is tied to the benchmark suite and tested configurations.
failures.response: Expand model and task diversity before claiming general performance improvements.

currentStatus.title: Active research system with a usable operator surface.
body: The evaluation harness, Lean promotion record, diagnostics, benchmark runners, and CLI are working. Research continues around model diversity, decomposition, autoresearch, and identifying configurations that generalize beyond a single suite.
